abstract: Selectively capturing carbon dioxide in the presence of moisture remains a significant challenge in atmospheric chemistry. In this study, we demonstrate that the interior of IRMOF-74-III can be covalently modified with primary amines (IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2) to achieve effective CO2 capture under 65% relative humidity. We present a comprehensive analysis of the synthesis, structural characterization, and gas adsorption properties of several functionalized IRMOF-74-III variants, including derivatives with -CH3, -NH2, -CH2NHBoc, -CH2NMeBoc, -CH2NH2, and -CH2NHMe groups. Using cross-polarization magic angle spinning (13)C NMR spectroscopy, we confirmed that CO2 chemically binds to the -CH2NH2 and -CH2NHMe derivatives via the formation of carbamate species. Adsorption isotherms and breakthrough experiments reveal that IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2 is particularly efficient, achieving a capacity of 3.2 mmol g⁻¹ at 800 Torr. Most importantly, this material effectively removes CO2 from wet nitrogen streams with a breakthrough time of 610 ± 10 s g⁻¹, all while maintaining the integrity of its crystalline framework.
